summary: Delaware deliverables remain substantively well-grounded. All seven load-bearing primary citations on delcode.delaware.gov (14 Del. C. §§ 2908, 2901, 2906, 2907, 508(b); 29 Del. C. §§ 6904(c), 6902) re-verified verbatim against live official text. All five field verdicts hold: no statutory max term for regular-district contracts; § 2908(a) first-offer-of-renewal + § 2908(d) annual letter-of-commitment; clean § 6904(c) exemption from Title 29 Ch. 69; § 508(b) charter carve-out; no separate special-ed contracting regime. Adversarial audit surfaces three items beyond Stage 1 QC: (a) the § 2901(c)(4) McKinney-Vento clause Stage 1 QC characterized as a general 'save-harmless' is in fact subsection-scoped — it limits only subsection (c) (Delaware Transit Corp annual bus passes), so the original Stage 1 deliverables' OMISSION of the clause is benign, but Stage 1 QC's framing of the clause as broad-framework support is itself imprecise (downgrading DE-C2-01 to a confirmation-only note); (b) Stage 1 QC's SB 272 description states a $1M threshold, but the floor-amended version reported in DE press raised the threshold to $5M for projects advertised after Dec. 31, 2026 — finding is non-load-bearing because SB 272 governs public-works construction PLAs, not transportation service contracts, but the figure is stale; (c) the official regulations.delaware.gov URL was re-tested and still renders header-only — Cornell LII Tier-2 substitution remains a genuine P2 hygiene issue. Six Stage 1 QC issues preserved (one with downgraded scope, one new DE-C3-02 for the SB 272 figure). Overall verdict: Minor (no P1 findings).

citationVerifications.citation: 14 Del. C. § 2908 (Renewal of transportation contracts; transfer; letters of commitment)
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Re-fetched delcode.delaware.gov/title14/c029/ via WebFetch. § 2908(a) verbatim: 'When a transportation contract is due to expire for a reason other than the fact that the bus described in said contract is of such vintage that it can no longer be used to transport pupils, a renewal of said contract shall be first offered to the school bus contractor who is party to the expiring contract.' § 2908(b) agency-takeover backstop confirmed. § 2908(c) contract-transfer with local board approval confirmed. § 2908(d) letter-of-commitment authority confirmed. Source-law trail confirmed verbatim: 62 Del. Laws c. 292; 63 Del. Laws c. 184; 66 Del. Laws c. 303; 67 Del. Laws c. 47; 68 Del. Laws c. 74; 70 Del. Laws c. 59 (§§ 1, 3); 71 Del. Laws c. 180 (§ 142). URL is the Chapter 29 portal index (Delaware Code Online uses on-page section anchors rather than per-section URLs — canonical official structure). Section content matches the citation. Verdict: verified.

citationVerifications.citation: 14 Del. C. § 2901(b) (Award of school transportation contracts) and § 2901(c)
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Re-fetched. § 2901(b) award criteria (financial stability, insurability, record of service, business resume) verified verbatim. § 2901(c) verified verbatim — it addresses Delaware Transit Corporation annual student bus passes for grades 6-12 (NOT general procurement rules). § 2901(c)(4) verbatim: 'This subsection does not limit the manner in which schools provide transportation for students under the McKinney-Vento Homeless Education Assistance Improvement Act, 42 U.S.C. §§ 11431 through 11435.' IMPORTANT SCOPE CORRECTION to Stage 1 QC: the clause is scoped to 'this subsection' — it preserves McKinney-Vento flexibility ONLY for the public-transit-pass scheme of subsection (c), not as a general save-harmless across § 2901. Source-law trail through 82 Del. Laws c. 188 (2019) confirmed. The deliverables' omission of (c)(4) remains acceptable — its narrow public-transit-pass scope does not change the special-ed contracting analysis.

citationVerifications.citation: 14 Del. C. § 2906 (Transportation of pupils to special schools)
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Re-fetched. § 2906 confirmed funding-only (multi-district special-school transportation funding), no procurement/contract-term language. Source-law: 62 Del. Laws c. 68 § 46; 62 Del. Laws c. 173 § 1. Matches deliverables.
Re-fetched. § 2907 confirms 14-model-year cap if safety-compliant + board approval. Source-law: 62 Del. Laws c. 275 § 1; 71 Del. Laws c. 180 § 142; 77 Del. Laws c. 327 § 363. Matches deliverables.

Re-fetched delcode.delaware.gov/title14/c005/. § 508(b) verbatim text confirmed: (1) 'Publically bid the transportation routes, either alone or with other local education agencies.' (2) 'Negotiate a contract for 1 or more years for contractor payment for school transportation up to the maximum rate.' Fuel-adjustment provision confirmed. Multi-year funding mechanic confirmed: state funds guarantee year 1; subsequent years a local commitment until subsection (a) allocation is determined. Source-law trail: 70 Del. Laws c. 179; 71 Del. Laws c. 132; 73 Del. Laws c. 312; 75 Del. Laws c. 89; 76 Del. Laws c. 280; 78 Del. Laws c. 290; 82 Del. Laws c. 188; 84 Del. Laws c. 29 (2024). Matches deliverables.

citationVerifications.citation: 29 Del. C. § 6904(c) (State Procurement - exception for school transportation)
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Re-fetched. § 6904(c) verbatim: 'This chapter shall not apply to contracts for the transportation of school children. Such contracts shall be submitted to the Secretary of Education through the Department of Education Transportation Office for approval.' Matches deliverables exactly. No 2025-2026 amendment to § 6904(c) located via direct search of legis.delaware.gov.

citationVerifications.citation: 29 Del. C. § 6902 (Definitions - 'Covered agency' excludes school districts)
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Re-fetched. Definition confirms 'Covered agency' excludes school districts, Delaware Technical & Community College, Delaware State University, and Legislative Branch. Supports applicability analysis.

citationVerifications.citation: 14 Del. Admin. Code § 1150-4.0 (Conditions for School Bus Contractors)
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Cornell LII page re-fetched and resolves. 11 contractor-obligation subsections (4.1.1-4.1.11) confirmed covering compliance, communications, employee management, qualifications, medical reporting, route adherence. No term/renewal/rebid language — matches deliverables. CAVEAT (preserved from Stage 1 QC): Cornell LII is an UNOFFICIAL mirror; the OFFICIAL regulations.delaware.gov URL was re-tested in this audit and STILL renders header-only via WebFetch. Source-tier labeling as Tier 2 is correct as to source-type but the URL host is non-official.

Cornell LII page re-fetched and resolves. 7-year capital allowance verified verbatim: 'seven (7) years of capital allowances with the rate specified for' year of manufacture. 14th-year replacement confirmed. 9-year/130,000 mi confirmed. 190,000 mi confirmed. 10-year provision confirmed. Same Cornell-LII-vs-official-site caveat.

citationVerifications.citation: 14 Del. Admin. Code § 1150-23.0 (Transportation for Pupils with Disabilities)
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Cornell LII page re-fetched and resolves. Confirmed scope: private-placement eligibility, IDEA financial-aid scope, residential-program transportation, case-by-case reimbursement, IEP-required state-funded bus aides. No procurement/term/renewal language — matches deliverables. Same caveat.

citationVerifications.citation: Delaware DOE - School Transportation program pages
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: WebFetch returned truncated content on this audit's re-attempt (only stub returned). Firecrawl fallback unavailable (token expired during audit). Stage 1 QC reported Firecrawl-based verification of the '130,000 pupils / 1,800 buses / 19 districts / 25 charter schools' content; that re-verification is preserved as Stage 1 QC's record. This audit could not independently re-confirm via Firecrawl due to the token issue. Non-load-bearing for the five field verdicts.

citationVerifications.citation: Brandywine School District Transportation Services RFP - BSD22101-TRANSPORT
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: URL exists and served a 1.1MB PDF binary on this audit's re-attempt — existence and file-type confirmed. WebFetch could not parse the PDF inline; Firecrawl PDF parser unavailable on retry. Specific contract-term text (1-year initial + 2 one-year renewals + 3-month extension) not re-verified verbatim by this audit. Non-load-bearing — Tier-5 district practice.

citationVerifications.citation: Delaware SB 272 (153rd GA) - Title 14 / Title 29 PLA amendments (current-law check item)
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Re-fetched. Bill confirmed at status 'Out of Committee; placed on Ready List' as of 6/10/26. CORRECTION TO STAGE 1 QC: Stage 1 QC reported '$1M' threshold. The official bill page returned the as-introduced $1M threshold, but contemporaneous reporting (Delaware Senate Democrats, Delaware Business Times) indicates a floor amendment raised the threshold to $5M and added a two-union-contractors-per-craft trigger. The threshold figure ($1M vs $5M) is not load-bearing because SB 272 amends PLAs for school-district PUBLIC WORKS / CONSTRUCTION contracts, NOT pupil-transportation SERVICE contracts. Stage 1's substantive conclusion holds: SB 272 does not amend § 2901/§ 2906/§ 2907/§ 2908/§ 508(b)/§ 6904(c) or any pupil-transportation service-contract provision.

mismatches: scratch/delaware.json sourcesUnreachable lists 'law.justia.com (HTTP 403; superseded by official delcode.delaware.gov source)' but Justia is not a cited URL in the Delaware source-ledger — provenance residue from prior stages, not a substantive cross-file mismatch.

scratch/delaware.json sourcesUnreachable lists 'bidcondocs.delaware.gov/DOE/DOE26002-BUSROUTE-rfp.pdf' as binary/non-load-bearing, but this URL appears NEITHER in the source-ledger Delaware rows NOR in research-findings 'Sources Consulted' for Delaware. It is referenced only descriptively in research-findings Exceptions and in verification-notes provenance. Cross-file asymmetry: described in prose but not entered in the ledger.

research-findings Delaware Exceptions paragraph references 'DOE-administered DOE26002-BUSROUTE' as one of 'two district RFPs reviewed' but only Brandywine BSD22101-TRANSPORT is formally entered in the source-ledger.

scratch/delaware.json reports 'adversarialOverallVerdict' as a compound 'confirmed (5/5 fields)' label while verification-notes Provenance table reports five separate per-field 'confirmed' verdicts — structural/format-only.

Stage 1 QC summary characterized § 2901(c)(4) as a McKinney-Vento save-harmless clause supporting the overall framework; live re-fetch confirms the clause is subsection-scoped (preserves McKinney-Vento only within subsection (c) on Delaware Transit Corp bus passes). Substantively the deliverables' omission of the clause is fine; Stage 1 QC's framing in DE-C2-01 was over-broad.

issues.evidence: Three regulatory citations (14 Del. Admin. Code §§ 1150-4.0, 1150-21.0, 1150-23.0) point to Cornell LII (https://www.law.cornell.edu/regulations/delaware/14-Del-Admin-Code-SS-1150-*), an UNOFFICIAL secondary mirror. The OFFICIAL source is regulations.delaware.gov/AdminCode/title14/1100/1150.shtml; this audit RE-CONFIRMED that the official URL renders header-only via WebFetch (returns 'Delaware Regulations' header with no regulation body). Substantive text on Cornell LII matches the regulation. Tier-2 'State administrative code' citation discipline calls for an official host.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Add the official regulations.delaware.gov URL (or the regulations.delaware.gov API endpoint that does render full text) alongside the Cornell LII URL, and relabel Cornell LII explicitly as a 'mirror.' If the official URL continues to render header-only via standard fetch, document that as a limitation rather than substitute the mirror silently.

issues.evidence: Primary URLs for § 2908 (https://delcode.delaware.gov/title14/c029/), § 508 (https://delcode.delaware.gov/title14/c005/), and § 6904(c) (https://delcode.delaware.gov/title29/c069/sc01/index.html) are chapter-portal URLs, not section-pinpoint URLs. They DO resolve and contain the cited sections — Delaware Code Online uses on-page #section anchors as its canonical structure. No mismatch on the law side; reader must scroll.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Optional: append an on-page anchor (e.g., '...c029/index.shtml#2908') if the site supports direct section linking. Acceptable as-is given canonical official structure.

issues.evidence: Research-findings Delaware Exceptions paragraph references 'DOE-administered DOE26002-BUSROUTE' as one of 'two district RFPs reviewed,' but DOE26002 is NOT entered as a source row in the Delaware section of source-ledger.md (only Brandywine BSD22101-TRANSPORT is). Verification-notes lists DOE26002 in provenance but the URL is not cited as a source.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Either add a Tier-5 source-ledger row for DOE26002 with the bidcondocs.delaware.gov/DOE/DOE26002-BUSROUTE-rfp.pdf URL, or remove the descriptive reference to keep cross-file citations symmetric.

issues.evidence: 14 Del. C. § 2901(c)(4) verbatim: 'This subsection does not limit the manner in which schools provide transportation for students under the McKinney-Vento Homeless Education Assistance Improvement Act, 42 U.S.C. §§ 11431 through 11435.' Stage 1 QC characterized this as a McKinney-Vento save-harmless clause that supports the deliverables' framework finding. ADVERSARIAL CORRECTION: the clause is SUBSECTION-SCOPED ('this subsection') and limits only § 2901(c) (Delaware Transit Corporation annual student bus-pass scheme for grades 6-12), not the broader § 2901/§ 2908 framework. The deliverables' OMISSION of the clause is therefore benign — it does not bear on the regular-route procurement analysis. Stage 1 QC's enrichment recommendation should be re-scoped: if added at all, the clause belongs in a discussion of the bus-pass alternative, not in the special-ed paragraph as a general McKinney-Vento exception.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Optional and narrow: if the special-ed paragraph mentions McKinney-Vento at all, note that § 2901(c)(4) preserves McKinney-Vento flexibility WITHIN the Delaware Transit Corp bus-pass alternative under § 2901(c), without creating any separate state procurement regime. The deliverables' existing approach (no mention) is also defensible.

issues.evidence: Live current-law check confirms no 2025-2026 amendment to § 2908, § 2901, § 2906, § 2907, § 508(b), or § 6904(c). SB 272 (153rd GA, Out of Committee 6/10/26) amends Title 14 and Title 29 only with respect to Project Labor Agreements for school-district public-works contracts — does not reach transportation service contracts. Deliverables' currency check is correct.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Optional: in verification-notes session-law check, name SB 272 (153rd GA) explicitly as reviewed-and-excluded for completeness.

issues.evidence: NEW ISSUE (not in Stage 1 QC). Stage 1 QC summary describes SB 272 as 'addresses only public-works PLAs >= $1M for projects advertised after Dec. 31, 2026.' Live re-fetch of legis.delaware.gov bill page returned the as-introduced $1M threshold, but contemporaneous Delaware media (Senate Democrats news release; Delaware Business Times; Cape Gazette) reports a floor amendment raised the threshold to $5M and added a two-union-contractors-per-craft trigger. Stage 1 QC's $1M figure is therefore likely STALE relative to the bill's current text on the floor. Substantively non-load-bearing because SB 272 governs public-works construction, not transportation service contracts. Note: this issue is internal to the Stage 1 QC pack's narrative; the deliverables themselves do not mention a dollar threshold for SB 272.
issues.recommendedRemediation: If SB 272 is named in any final deliverable update, cite the floor-amended figure ($5M, plus two-union-contractors-per-craft trigger) rather than the as-introduced $1M, and verify against the engrossed bill text rather than the as-introduced version. No remediation required if SB 272 is not named in the deliverables.

issues.evidence: scratch/delaware.json sourcesUnreachable lists 'law.justia.com (HTTP 403; superseded by official delcode.delaware.gov source)' and 'bidcondocs.delaware.gov/DOE/DOE26002-BUSROUTE-rfp.pdf (binary; existence confirmed; non-load-bearing)'. Neither Justia nor DOE26002 is a cited URL in the Delaware source-ledger. Listing them as 'unreachable' is provenance residue from substitution history, not a record of unreachable cited sources.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Optional: rename the JSON field to 'sourcesSubstituted' or 'sourcesDeprecated' to reflect that these were superseded, not unreachable cited sources.

issues.evidence: Three regulatory citations rest on Cornell LII, a secondary/mirror source. Source-tier discipline calls for Tier-2 administrative-code findings to cite the OFFICIAL administrative code (regulations.delaware.gov) when available. This audit re-confirmed that the official URL renders header-only via WebFetch — a host-level limitation, not a finding-level defect. None of the regulations is itself load-bearing for max-term/rebid/renewal (the regulations contain no term/renewal/rebid provisions and serve only as supporting context for the bus-age horizon and special-ed analyses), which prevents this from being P1.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Replace or supplement Cornell LII URLs with the official Delaware regulations URL (or the API/PDF endpoint that renders text), and explicitly note Cornell LII as a 'mirror' where the official site is fetch-resistant.

issues.evidence: Live disconfirming searches (AG opinions, AOA reports, DOE bulletins, DSBA guidance, district policy manuals) located nothing authoritative documenting either uniformity or interpretive divergence — confirming the 'Unknown' verdict. The 1974 AG opinion located via search addresses use of state-allocated buses for non-pupil purposes, not interpretive divergence in § 2908 application. Brandywine RFP existence confirmed (1.1MB PDF served at bidcondocs.delaware.gov); contract-term verbatim not re-parsed by this audit (PDF parser unavailable on retry). Field does not turn on the contract-term specifics.
issues.recommendedRemediation: No remediation required for the finding. Optional: formally enter DOE26002-BUSROUTE in the source-ledger as a Tier-5 row if the deliverables are going to reference it descriptively (overlaps with DE-C4-01).
